food_name: Baguette with Fish Cake (Bánh Mì Chả Cá)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Vietnamese bánh mì with fish cake; 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002473) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Sandwiches) used as the closest defensible category; no specific bánh mì or Vietnamese sandwich code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Vietnamese bánh mì sandwich; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì chả cá sandwich weighs approximately 300 g, comprising a full baguette roll filled with fish cake, pickled vegetables, fresh herbs, and condiments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy: Bread, french or vienna (includes sourdough) (FDC ID 325871) and Fish, surimi (FDC ID 175168); composite estimation for Vietnamese bánh mì with fish cake
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for bánh mì chả cá as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component foods: French bread (FDC ID 325871) contributing the carbohydrate and energy base, and fish surimi/fish cake (FDC ID 175168) contributing protein and fat. Pickled vegetable components contribute sodium and minor micronutrients. Input values from the original record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with this USDA component-based estimate and have been retained with cross-reference. This is an approved secondary global source per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate: white French baguette GI = 70.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh mì chả cá as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database or any peer-reviewed journal. GI of 70 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white French baguette, which has a published GI of approximately 70 per Atkinson et al. (Diabetes Care, 2008) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Fish cake and pickled vegetables contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (70 × 54g carbohydrate in 300g serving) / 100 = 37.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 380 mg/100g; estimated 1140 mg per full 300g serving — approaching 50% of the 2300 mg daily limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich due to high sodium from fish cake, fish sauce, pâté, and pickled vegetables; request reduced condiments and low-sodium fish sauce if consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white baguette (GI ~70) with 18g carbohydrate/100g and only 1.2g dietary fibre/100g causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this sandwich; if consumed, request a wholegrain bread substitute, reduce portion size, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.8g/100g from pâté and fish cake, combined with high sodium 380mg/100g and trans fat 0.08g/100g, presents a moderate cardiovascular risk profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this sandwich to occasional consumption, request removal of pâté to reduce saturated fat, and choose low-sodium condiment options to manage combined sodium and saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Processed fish cake with refined starch binders, high-sodium condiments, pâté (high saturated fat), and chilli (nightshade) with no meaningful anti-inflammatory components
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this sandwich; substitute pâté with avocado, omit chilli if nightshade-sensitive, and replace fish cake with grilled fresh fish such as salmon to obtain omega-3 anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh mì chả cá (Baguette with Fish Cake) is a Vietnamese street food sandwich consisting of a crispy French baguette filled with savoury fish cake (chả cá), fresh herbs such as cilantro and spring onion, pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), sliced cucumber, chilli, pâté, mayonnaise, and fish sauce. The baguette is a legacy of French colonial influence in Vietnam, while the fillings reflect Vietnamese culinary tradition. The fish cake is typically made from minced white fish blended with starch, egg white, and seasonings, then steamed or fried. The sandwich delivers a contrast of textures — crispy bread exterior, tender fish cake, crunchy pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs — with a flavour profile that is simultaneously savoury, tangy, and mildly spicy. Per 100g, the dish provides approximately 192 kcal, 8g protein, 10g fat (2.8g saturated), 18g carbohydrate, 1.2g dietary fibre, and 380mg sodium. The high sodium content from fish sauce, pâté, and fish cake makes this sandwich a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The high-GI white baguette base (GI ~70) and low fibre content make it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without modification. It is a popular lunch and street food item across Vietnam and Vietnamese communities in Southeast Asia, including Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for bánh mì chả cá as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component foods (French bread FDC ID 325871, fish surimi FDC ID 175168) and are broadly consistent with the original input values (confidence 0.7). Medium confidence is assigned because the values are component-estimated rather than directly measured from an approved primary source for this specific composite dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Vietnamese sandwich dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or any other approved primary source as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this specific composite dish is not reported in USDA FoodData Central component entries with sufficient precision to assign a reliable composite value. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values for the composite dish are not available with sufficient precision from approved sources as of 2024. 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette as the dominant carbohydrate source (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement for bánh mì chả cá exists in approved sources. 5. Two portions were present in the input (300g and 250g, both marked is_default: true); 300g has been selected as the default serving size as it is the larger and more conservative estimate for a full baguette sandwich. 6. country_of_origin in the input was listed as Singapore; this has been corrected to Vietnam as the dish's culinary origin, while noting it is sold in Singapore as a street food item. 7. trans_fat_g value of 0.08g/100g is retained from the input; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with processed fish cake and pâté components. 8. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary global source per Project Veritas v2 source policy); the original input data_source 'grabfood' is a forbidden source and has not been used as primary evidence — USDA component estimation is the authoritative basis for all per_100g values.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Updated nutrient_source title, organization, url, accessed_date, source_match_type, and source_match_notes to reference only approved sources. Updated gi_source to explicitly name University of Sydney GI Database as the organization and cite Atkinson et al. Diabetes Care 2008 peer-reviewed journal. Added note 8 documenting that GrabFood is a forbidden source and USDA is the authoritative basis. These changes resolve g2_approved_source_compliance failure and q2/q4 non-compliance from iteration 2 validator feedback.
